evolution_form.description: Design a following monster's evolved form from its lived history and optional player guidance: new species, root-keeping name, updated invented lineage
evolution_form.prompt_template:
A monster that follows the adventuring party is about to EVOLVE at the home-base sanctuary - the big, earned leap its whole journey has been building toward. You design the form it becomes.

========== CONTEXT ==========

--- The monster as it is now ---
{monster_details}

--- What it has lived through ---
{monster_memories}

--- Its fixed lineage (never changes) ---
{locked_lineage}

--- The moment ---
{stage_note}

--- The player's guidance ---
{player_guidance}

========== YOUR TASK ==========
Design the evolved form. It must be a clear DESCENDANT of what it is - grown from its history, wishes, and scars (and the player's guidance, when given) - never a different creature:
1. species - the new species name (1-3 words), evolved from the current one (e.g. Pebble Golem -> Basalt Colossus).
2. evolved_name - its personal name, evolved but still recognizable: it MUST contain part of the current name (e.g. Rokk -> Rokkarath). Keep the current name unchanged if it already fits the new form.
3. family - an updated invented family within its fixed lineage; null to keep the current one.
4. genus - an updated invented genus; null to keep.
5. race_label - a short display label for the new form (e.g. 'Colossus'); null to keep.
6. size_class - ONLY if the new body truly changes size: exactly one of "tiny", "small", "medium", "large", "huge", "colossal"; else null.
7. form_theme - one short phrase for the essence of this change (e.g. 'patience hardened into living stone').

========== RESPONSE FORMAT ==========
Respond with ONLY valid JSON in exactly this format:
{{"species": "Basalt Colossus", "evolved_name": "Rokkarath", "family": null, "genus": null, "race_label": "Colossus", "size_class": null, "form_theme": "One short phrase."}}

JSON:

evolution_narration.description: Streamed narration of an evolution ceremony at the home-base sanctuary

evolution_persona.description: How a monster's inner life shifts with its evolved body - voice, battle cry, goals; the soul itself is untouchable
evolution_persona.prompt_template:
A monster has just EVOLVED at the party's home base. Same soul, new body. You decide how its inner life settles into the new form.

========== CONTEXT ==========

--- The monster (in its new form) ---
{monster_details}

--- The transformation it just went through ---
{transformation_facts}

--- The player's guidance ---
{player_guidance}

========== YOUR TASK ==========
Its core wish, its secret, and its bonds DO NOT change - evolution remakes the body, not the soul. Decide only what the new form reshapes:
1. battle_line - its battle cry reworded for the new form (similar length to the old one); null to keep it.
2. speech_style - its voice after the change, one line (deeper, calmer, wilder...); null if unchanged.
3. goals - 1-3 updated goals as a list, grown from the old ones; null to keep them.
4. motivations - one line: what drives it now; null to keep.
5. memory_note - one past-tense sentence it will remember forever about the moment it evolved.

========== RESPONSE FORMAT ==========
Respond with ONLY valid JSON in exactly this format:
{{"battle_line": "One line or null.", "speech_style": "One line or null.", "goals": ["first goal", "second goal"], "motivations": "One line or null.", "memory_note": "One past-tense sentence."}}

JSON:

evolution_prose.description: The evolved monster's new description, a fresh backstory chapter, and the appearance block that drives its new card art
evolution_prose.prompt_template:
A monster has just EVOLVED at the party's home base. You write the words (and the artist's brief) for its new form.

========== CONTEXT ==========

--- The monster (identity and stats already updated) ---
{monster_details}

--- The transformation ---
{transformation_facts}

--- How its inner life shifted ---
{persona_shift_facts}

--- How it USED to look ---
{old_visual_description}

--- The player's guidance ---
{player_guidance}

========== YOUR TASK ==========
1. description - 2-3 sentences: the evolved creature as it now is (present tense).
2. backstory_addendum - 2-4 sentences: the NEW chapter of its story - the evolution and what it means. Do NOT retell its old backstory; this is appended to it.
3. visual_description - 2-3 sentences for the card artist: the new body, clearly DESCENDED from how it used to look (keep what makes it recognizable, evolve the rest).
4. primary_colors - 2-4 colors of the new form.
5. distinguishing_features - 2-4 short visual features.

========== RESPONSE FORMAT ==========
Respond with ONLY valid JSON in exactly this format:
{{"description": "2-3 sentences.", "backstory_addendum": "2-4 sentences.", "visual_description": "2-3 sentences.", "primary_colors": ["obsidian black", "ember orange"], "distinguishing_features": ["molten seams across its shell", "a crown of basalt spikes"]}}

JSON:

evolution_abilities.description: Which abilities evolve with the body: up to two rewords (same power, evolved expression) and whether the new form earned one signature ability
evolution_abilities.prompt_template:
A monster has just EVOLVED at the party's home base. Its abilities are part of who it is - they come along, but the new body may express them differently.

========== CONTEXT ==========

--- The monster (in its new form) ---
{monster_details}

--- The transformation ---
{transformation_facts}

--- Its current abilities (exact names) ---
{existing_abilities_text}

--- The player's guidance ---
{player_guidance}

========== YOUR TASK ==========
1. reword_1 - the EXACT name of ONE existing ability whose expression evolves with the body; or "none".
2. reword_1_new_name - its evolved name (e.g. 'Stone Wall' -> 'Basalt Rampart'); null to keep the name.
3. reword_1_description - its new description: SIMILAR LENGTH to the old one, never longer, SAME power - only the expression evolves.
4. reword_2 / reword_2_new_name / reword_2_description - optionally a second ability, same rules; reword_2 "none" if not.
5. new_ability - "yes" ONLY if the new form demands one signature trick that NONE of its current abilities can express; else "no".
6. ability_theme - if new_ability is yes: one short phrase for what that signature ability must do; else null.

========== RESPONSE FORMAT ==========
Respond with ONLY valid JSON in exactly this format:
{{"reword_1": "Stone Wall", "reword_1_new_name": "Basalt Rampart", "reword_1_description": "Similar-length description.", "reword_2": "none", "reword_2_new_name": null, "reword_2_description": null, "new_ability": "no", "ability_theme": null}}

JSON:
